role: The pure-read projection engine that maps stored events into polar coordinates and sector layouts from a variable binding, producing the data model that a surface will render.
capabilities: Discovers and loads binding lenses from disk at runtime
Resolves angular sectors from a binding's angle_from source (kind, kind-group, registry, graph, nucleation types+zones)
Computes polar coordinates (theta, r) for each event using time, address-tree, semantic cosine, two-pole separator, or nucleation placement
Provides human-readable kind/sector names and meanings from declared registries with humanized fallback
Strips machine tokens from operator-facing summaries while preserving genuine human content for chat/annotation/trial.turn
Computes the two-gravity separation report (pole distinctness, spread, Spearman, balance)
Computes the 20/80 nucleation report (type membership, candidate birth, dissolution candidates)
Surfaces directed typed edges between sectors for chord rendering
Supports a temporal scrubber (?at=) and spatial re-centring by address
Computes dyadic grid cells and ring counts from address paths
substance_why: The file implements a complete, multi-mode projection engine with binding discovery, sector resolution, several radius strategies, separation/nucleation analytics, humanization, and full point output — not a stub.
interactions_present: None: this is a pure data-transformation module with no UI or user-event wiring
interactions_missing: No user-facing binding/lens selector or configuration UI
No live scrubber control or time-slider interaction
No point selection, hover, drill-in, or tooltip rendering
No error-to-user feedback for malformed events or missing vectors
No loading state or progress indication for heavy analytics
No interactive re-centring or pole-picking UI
No inspector/legend interaction beyond emitted metadata
performance_risks: O(N*T) cosine scans for semantic/separator/nucleation modes over all events and type/pole vectors
Nucleation agglomerate is O(n³) even though bounded by `cap`
Binding discovery rescans and dynamically imports files on every no-arg call
Repeated SHA-256 hashing and regex processing per event in the hot loop
No caching of resolved sectors, humanized labels, or registry metadata
Vector dimension mismatch raises hard errors rather than degrading gracefully
reuse_why: The core projection concepts (binding-driven sectors, variable radius, separation/nucleation analytics, operator-law humanization) are domain-specific and should be preserved, but the monolithic implementation needs modularization and clearer separation of concerns for the redesign.
decomposition_hints: Split binding discovery/loading into its own module (runtime/bindings/registry.py)
Split sector resolution into a strategy per angle_from source (kind, kind-group, registry, graph, nucleation)
Split radius computation into pluggable radius modes (time, address, semantic, separator, nucleation)
Extract humanization/legibility helpers (kind/sector names, summary sanitization) into a dedicated runtime/humanize module
Move separation_report and nucleation_report analytics into runtime/analytics/ or runtime/reports/
Separate geometry/layout concerns (theta, rings, grid cells, edges) from data resolution
